purpose: Defines the !health slash command — comprehensive project health audit. Checks documentation health (CLAUDE.md presence/coverage, .synapse/toc.md presence and consistency with disk state, _commands/ presence), dependency health (node_modules existence, lock files, requirements.txt/go.mod/Cargo.toml status), and cross-layer health (frontend API references match backend handlers, .env.example completeness, type consistency across layers). Supports --quick flag to skip deep cross-layer analysis. Outputs Documentation/Git Status/Dependencies/Cross-Layer Issues/Recommendations table prioritized critical-first. Read-only.
gotchas: TOC consistency check is bidirectional: directories on disk missing from TOC AND entries in TOC pointing to deleted paths — both must be flagged.
--quick flag skips Step 3 (cross-layer) — only do this when speed matters; cross-layer drift is the most valuable signal.
Use Glob/Grep for quick checks — do NOT deep-read files unnecessarily.
Prioritization matters: missing CLAUDE.md / merge conflicts / broken imports are critical; stale TOC / missing .env.example are warnings.
patterns: Section-by-section audit: docs → deps → cross-layer → recommendations.
Severity-ordered output: critical first, warnings last.
conventions: Checklist format ([ ] items) for each section.
